summary: First pass at matching Jigglypuff/Purin fighter code. The PR moved a large amount of `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpurin.s` into `src/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftpurin.c`, replacing some simple routines with C and preserving many remaining hard cases as inline `asm unk_t` blocks under `#pragma push`/`pop`. It also deleted the old baselib `mtx.s` assembly file, adjusted `HSD_MtxSRT` naming in C, relocated many headers from `include/` to `src/`, changed include search paths, and added focused effect/baselib/Dolphin headers needed by the ftPurin translation. PR body only says it resolves #586; there were no review comments in the slice.

key_files.reason: Main translation target; grew by about 6800 lines with ftPurin C routines, inline asm stubs, imports, callbacks, action-state setup, and special-move code.
Original external assembly was reduced from roughly 6400 text lines to a small remaining assembly/data tail, including float/double constants.
Baselib matrix code cleanup; `HSD_MtxSRT` parameter names were clarified from generic vec names to scale/rotation/translation/scale2.
Deleted old external assembly file after the corresponding baselib matrix code was represented in C/source form.
Include system was changed to use `src` as the system include directory, and assembler includes were changed from `-I include` to `-I asm`.
Renamed from `include/macros.inc`, matching the Makefile change that treats assembly includes as assembly-owned.
Introduced central primitive typedefs and placeholder macros such as `STRUCT_PLACEHOLDER`, `CALLBACK_PLACEHOLDER`, and `UNK_FUNC`.
Replaced the old include-path Dolphin types header with a `src` version including ctypes, assert helpers, paired-single register macros, and a temporary mtx types include.
New aggregate effect header used by fighter code.
Added prototypes for `efLib_PauseAll` and `efLib_ResumeAll`, used by ftPurin hitlag callbacks.
Added `ef_Spawn(s32, ...)` prototype with a note that the signature is fake because call sites use wrong arguments.
New umbrella baselib header that includes many baselib component headers now located under `src/sysdolphin/baselib`.

what_changed: Converted the ftPurin source file from a small lifecycle/item-handler file into the main home for Jigglypuff/Purin special-move implementation and the old assembly body.
Removed most of `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpurin.s` text and represented many not-yet-decompiled functions as inline `asm unk_t` blocks in `ftpurin.c`.

Added real C for some straightforward ftPurin routines, including `lbl_8013C94C`, `ftPurin_SpecialHi_SetVars`, `ftPurin_SpecialHi_SetActionFromFacingDirection`, `ftPurin_SpecialHi_StartAction`, `ftPurin_SpecialAirHi_StartAction`, and `func_8013CD34`.

Used ftPurin callback fields explicitly: `fp->cb.x21D4_callback_EnterHitlag = efLib_PauseAll`, `fp->cb.x21D8_callback_ExitHitlag = efLib_ResumeAll`, and `fp->cb.x21BC_callback_Accessory4` for accessory callbacks.
Made ftPurin's SpecialHi action selection depend on `x2C_facing_direction`, selecting action IDs 365/367 for ground and 366/368 for air.
Preserved behavior in hard functions by leaving inline assembly blocks rather than forcing fragile C rewrites.
Deleted `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.s` and kept baselib matrix code in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.c`.
Renamed `HSD_MtxSRT` parameters from generic `m`, `vec1`, `vec2`, `vec3`, `vec4` to `mtx`, `scale1`, `rotation`, `translation`, `scale2`, clarifying the transform math without changing the algorithm.
Moved many headers from `include/` to `src/`, including Dolphin, MSL/runtime, common structs, functions, and sysdolphin baselib headers.
Changed build include behavior: `SYSTEM_INCLUDE_DIRS := src`, `ASFLAGS := -mgekko -I asm`, and renamed `include/macros.inc` to `asm/macros.inc`.
Added effect headers so fighter code can include effect APIs directly instead of keeping those declarations in the global `functions.h`.
Removed a set of broad sysdolphin/effect declarations from `functions.h` after moving them into narrower subsystem headers.
smart_moves: Staged the decomp by moving assembly into the C translation unit as inline asm, allowing the file to be integrated, included, typed, and incrementally decompiled without losing matching behavior.
Extracted small, repeated C helpers for ftPurin SpecialHi setup and facing-direction action selection instead of duplicating action-state boilerplate.
Introduced focused effect headers (`ef.h`, `eflib.h`, `efsync.h`) to reduce reliance on the monolithic `functions.h` declaration dump.
Made callback assignments use struct field names, which documents what offsets like `0x21BC`, `0x21D4`, and `0x21D8` represent.
Kept suspicious or incomplete signatures explicit: `ef_Spawn` is declared variadic and annotated as a fake signature because call sites use wrong arguments.
Moved assembler-only macros into `asm/macros.inc` and updated `ASFLAGS`, separating C include hygiene from assembly include needs.
Improved local variable and parameter names in matrix code where semantics were clear (`scale1`, `rotation`, `translation`, `scale2`) without overclaiming unknown fighter symbols.
decomp_lessons: A useful pass-1 strategy for large fighter files is to relocate external `.s` into the `.c` file as inline asm, then peel off small matching C functions one at a time.
When a function is not ready to decompile, preserving it as `asm unk_t` with `nofralloc` can keep progress buildable and searchable while avoiding accidental non-matches.
Callback field names in shared structs are high leverage: once `x21BC_callback_Accessory4` and hitlag callback fields are named, related fighter code becomes easier to translate.
Action-state helper wrappers are a good way to encode repeated `Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC` patterns, especially when only action IDs differ by grounded/airborne or facing direction.
Header relocation can be necessary decomp infrastructure: moving authoritative headers under `src` made C include paths simpler and reduced the need for global declarations.

Do not hide uncertainty in headers. This PR explicitly left notes like `not correct length`, `fake signature`, and `TODO! DO NOT LEAVE THIS IN!`, which is preferable to pretending the declarations are final.
Rename parameters only when the assembly/C behavior clearly supports the semantic role; `HSD_MtxSRT`'s scale/rotation/translation naming is a safe example.
naming_conventions: Character-specific exported functions use the `ftPurin_` prefix, e.g. `ftPurin_OnLoad`, `ftPurin_SpecialHi_StartAction`, and `ftPurin_SpecialAirHi_StartAction`.
Unknown address-based routines remain as `lbl_8013....` or `func_8013....` until semantics are known.
Static inline helpers in fighter files can use descriptive names with subsystem and behavior, e.g. `ftPurin_SpecialHi_SetVars` and `ftPurin_SpecialHi_SetActionFromFacingDirection`.
Callback fields are named with both offset and purpose, e.g. `x21D4_callback_EnterHitlag`, `x21D8_callback_ExitHitlag`, and `x21BC_callback_Accessory4`.
Sysdolphin baselib headers adopted all-caps include guards like `SYSDOLPHIN_BASELIB_COBJ_H` instead of older short guards like `_cobj_h_`.
Matrix parameter names should reflect transform roles: `mtx`, `scale1`, `rotation`, `translation`, and `scale2`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved bulk assembly from `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpurin.s` into `ftpurin.c` using inline `asm unk_t` functions surrounded by `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop`.
Used `nofralloc` in inline asm blocks to preserve hand-written prologues/epilogues.
Kept `// clang-format off` around inline asm to prevent formatting changes that could harm readability or tooling.
Declared external float constants such as `lbl_804D9C10`, `lbl_804D9C14`, and `lbl_804D9C18` for use by matched C around action-state changes.
Changed some assembly data constants from raw `.4byte` pairs to `.double` literals for known double values.
Adjusted assembler include path to `-I asm` after moving `macros.inc` under `asm/`.
Used variadic/fake prototypes for uncertain ABI-heavy calls like `ef_Spawn` to keep call sites compiling until exact signatures are known.

review_feedback.text: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #586 for the exact ftPurin matching target and any context not included in this PR body.
Search later PRs for removal of ftPurin inline `asm unk_t` blocks introduced here.
Search later PRs for corrections to the fake variadic `ef_Spawn` signature in `efsync.h`.
Search later PRs for cleanup of the `TODO! DO NOT LEAVE THIS IN!` include in `src/dolphin/types.h`.
Compare later `ftPurin` PRs for naming upgrades from `lbl_8013...`/`func_8013...` to semantic special-move function names.
Check follow-up header PRs to see whether the include relocation from `include/` to `src/` was kept or refined.
